The text discusses DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) attacks, a significant threat in modern networking security. DDoS attacks flood target systems with excessive traffic or manipulated packets to exhaust available bandwidth, network resources, or overload servers. There are three main categories of DDoS attacks:

1. Volumetric Attacks: Flooding Attacks and Reflection Attacks, which aim to overwhelm the target system with traffic.
